chief_complaint: A 55-year-old male with a 10-year history of type 2 diabetes presents with severe abdominal pain, vomiting, and polyuria.
history: The patient has been treated with empagliflozin, an SGLT-2 inhibitor, for the past 6 months. He has a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. His social history is significant for smoking and a sedentary lifestyle.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 140/90 mmHg, pulse 110 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min, temperature 37.5°C. Physical examination reveals abdominal tenderness and decreased level of consciousness.
labs: Blood glucose 420 mg/dL, pH 7.2, bicarbonate 15 mmol/L, potassium 4.5 mmol/L, ECG showing sinus tachycardia.
